sa:steps.rdfs:label: Choose Two Archetypes
sa:steps.sa:description: Select two archetypes that support your character concept. These choices grant you access to archetype features at their base cost, plus any free features defined by those archetypes. You may also purchase features from other archetypes, but they cost 1.5 times the standard price, rounded up to the nearest whole number (e.g., a 3-point feature costs 5 experience when purchased out-of-archetype).

sa:steps.rdfs:label: Choose a Race
sa:steps.sa:description: Select a race that fits your character's concept. Each race grants unique bonuses and abilities.

sa:steps.rdfs:label: Determine Ability Scores
sa:steps.sa:description: Assign scores to the six core abilities: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. You may use the standard array (15, 14, 13, 12, 10, 8) or the point buy system, which starts you with 27 points to distribute according to the Point Buy Table. Constraints: You may not lower a score below 8 or spend more than 9 points on a single ability.

sa:steps.rdfs:label: Calculate Final Statistics
sa:steps.sa:description:
Apply racial bonuses to your ability scores and record archetype features and proficiencies. Then calculate your character's derived statistics:

Health & Resources:
- Wounds = Constitution score
- Hit Points = Constitution modifier + 6
- Sanity = Wisdom score
- Spell Points (as specified by your archetypes, if any)
- Renown = Proficiency Bonus

Fixed Values:
- Proficiency Bonus = 2

Ability DCs (difficulty for checks made against your character):
- Melee: 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier
- Ranged: 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Dexterity modifier
- Spell: 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Casting Ability modifier

sa:steps.rdfs:label: Advancement
sa:steps.sa:description:
You earn experience by completing quests and challenges. Spend this experience immediately to purchase features—archetype features, cross-archetype features, or general features—as you earn it rather than saving it for "later" opportunities. You must have earned the experience through gameplay to be eligible to purchase; out-of-archetype features cost 1.5 times their base price.

Example: An Armsman/Arcanist purchases 'Quick and Silent' from the Recon archetype (which they do not possess). Base cost is 3 experience; the 1.5× multiplier increases it to 4 experience total.
